original_hypothesis.title: Phantom fee DoS: _txfeeRate>0 with _feesFaucet==0 blocks transfers without collecting fees
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When tx fees are enabled but _feesFaucet is unset (address(0)), _payTxFee enforces balance >= amount+fee but skips actually transferring the fee (because it only _update()s if _feesFaucet != 0). This creates a phantom fee requirement that can freeze accounts that have exactly the amount but not amount+fee, even though no fee is collected.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a production-credible misconfiguration risk: operational scripts do not show post-deploy initialization for feesFaucet/fee rate. A mistake can cause widespread transfer failures.
deployment_assumptions: Users attempt transfers with balances between [amount, amount+fee)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script sets feesFaucet or tx fee rate; both are mutable post-deploy by ADMIN and could be mis-set during operations

proof_of_signal.pattern: _payTxFee checks balance >= txFees+txAmount; only executes _update(from,_feesFaucet,txFees) when _feesFaucet != address(0)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Fee balance sufficiency check is enforced even when fee sink is unset
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Until feesFaucet is set or txfeeRate reset

operational_questions: Are there runbooks that enforce setting feesFaucet before enabling fees?
Is there monitoring for FeeFaucetUpdated and TxFeeRateUpdated event sequencing?
How many wallets typically hold exact amounts (common in stablecoins)?
validation_requirements: Simulate feeRate>0 and feesFaucet=0; attempt transfers and observe revert thresholds
Check whether meta-tx transferWithAuthorization and transferFrom share same phantom requirement (they do via transferSanity/_payTxFee)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for or induce (via operational pressure) misconfiguration where feeRate enabled but faucet unset
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Exploit as DoS by targeting accounts with tight balances and causing systematic transaction failure (e.g., exchange withdrawals fail)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Widespread transfer failures; exchange integration outages; loss of confidence
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low: set feesFaucet or set fee rate to 0, but may require customer support and rollback of failed operations
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All transfer paths that pay tx fee

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: TxFeeRateUpdated to non-zero while FeeFaucetUpdated remains zero
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Spike in transfer reverts with BalanceTooLow and feesFaucet==0
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Systemic failures across users rather than targeted

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
